text: Endurance exercise can attenuate muscle hypertrophy and strength gains when combined with resistance training (the interference effect).
The specificity of training adaptation is determined by the volume, intensity, frequency, and mode of contractile activity.

Divergent exercise modes (endurance vs resistance) induce similar signalling and gene expression profiles in skeletal muscle of untrained or recreationally active individuals, with specificity emerging through training history.
The interference effect in concurrent training is likely caused by multiple integrated effectors or processes rather than a single isolated mechanism.

Prior training history modifies the molecular specificity of the adaptive response to different exercise modes, meaning trained individuals show more mode-specific responses than untrained individuals.
